ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general iced tea class (FOODON_03301103); no specific lemon-lime iced tea subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0KP covers ready-to-drink tea broadly; no lemon-lime iced tea specific code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for lemon-lime iced tea beverage identified.

preparation_method: flavoured with lemon and lime juice

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 ml (300 g) of iced lemon lime tea served in a glass or cup with ice.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon-lime flavored tea beverage, ready-to-drink (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods reference for unsweetened iced tea with citrus)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for lemon-lime iced tea as served at GyoGyo. Values estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for brewed black tea (FDC ID 171917) and lemon/lime juice contribution. The beverage is predominantly water with trace citrus juice and minimal sugar (restaurant version may contain added sugar; values reflect a lightly sweetened preparation typical of Japanese restaurant iced citrus tea). Energy and carbohydrate reflect a lightly sweetened version (~3 g sugar per 100 ml). Protein and fat are naturally zero for this beverage type.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of tea and tea-based beverages — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (plain tea, GI < 1)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed tea has a GI effectively of zero as it contains no glycemic carbohydrates. Lemon-lime iced tea with minimal added sugar has negligible glycemic impact. GI set to 1 (lowest valid positive sentinel) per Project Veritas convention for essentially zero-GI beverages. GL per 300 g serving = (1 × 3.0 × 3) / 100 = 0.09, rounded to 0.0. gi_category set to low per convention.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~2 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Iced lemon lime tea is very low in sodium (~6 mg per 300 ml serving) and poses minimal concern for individuals managing hypertension, though restaurant versions with added syrups should be checked for sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Iced lemon lime tea has a negligible glycemic index and low carbohydrate content (~3 g per 100 ml); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should confirm whether added sugar or syrup is used, as sweetened versions may raise postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and antioxidant polyphenols from tea
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Iced lemon lime tea contains no fat or cholesterol and provides tea polyphenols with potential cardioprotective antioxidant effects; it is generally suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease when consumed without excessive added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Lemon Lime Tea is a chilled beverage made from brewed tea (typically black or green tea) combined with fresh lemon juice, lime juice, water, and optionally a small amount of sugar or sweetener, served over ice. At GyoGyo (Funan, Singapore), this drink is offered as a refreshing accompaniment to Japanese cuisine such as sushi, tempura, and grilled fish. The flavour profile is bright, tangy, and lightly sweet, with the citrus notes of lemon and lime balancing the mild bitterness of the tea base. The texture is thin and watery, characteristic of a dilute iced tea. Nutritionally, this beverage is very low in calories (approximately 12 kcal per 100 ml), contains negligible protein and fat (both naturally zero), and provides a small amount of carbohydrate primarily from natural citrus sugars and any added sweetener. It contributes a modest amount of vitamin C from the citrus juice. The glycemic index is effectively zero, making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is very low, making it appropriate for those monitoring sodium intake. The beverage contains tea polyphenols and flavonoids that may offer antioxidant benefits. It is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Clinically, it is a low-concern beverage for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease, provided added sugar is kept minimal.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific restaurant item (957D Iced Lemon Lime Tea at GyoGyo) exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for brewed tea and citrus juice components, representing a lightly sweetened iced citrus tea. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain tea. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as this is a tea-based beverage containing no protein-contributing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero as this beverage contains no fat-contributing ingredients. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero for this beverage type. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as the beverage is a liquid with no fibre. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no significant glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 reflecting negligible glycemic impact per 300 g serving. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA reference for this beverage type. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. The food_name '957D Iced Lemon Lime Tea(D)' from field survey is a menu code; canonical English name normalised to 'Iced Lemon Lime Tea'.
